title: Linear Algebra Unit 1: Vectors and Linear Systems

nodes.title: Vector operations
nodes.description: Vector addition, scalar multiplication, and their geometric meaning.

nodes.title: Linear combinations
nodes.description: Forming a vector as a weighted sum of other vectors.

nodes.title: Span
nodes.description: The set of all linear combinations of a set of vectors.

nodes.title: Matrix-vector product as a combination of columns
nodes.description: A x is the linear combination of the columns of A weighted by the entries of x.

nodes.title: Row picture and column picture
nodes.description: A linear system read as intersecting hyperplanes and as a combination of columns, two views of the same system.

nodes.title: Linear system as A x = b
nodes.description: Expressing a system of linear equations in matrix form.

nodes.title: Gaussian elimination to row echelon form
nodes.description: Reducing a system by elementary row operations.

nodes.title: Reduced row echelon form and pivots
nodes.description: The unique reduced form and identification of pivot positions.

nodes.title: Free variables
nodes.description: Identifying free variables from non-pivot columns and parameterizing.

nodes.title: Existence and uniqueness
nodes.description: Classifying a solution set as none, one, or infinitely many from the pivots.

nodes.title: Homogeneous systems
nodes.description: Systems A x = 0 and the always-present trivial solution.

misconceptions.name: Dimension-blind product
misconceptions.description: Multiplies matrix by vector row-by-row or ignores shape compatibility.

misconceptions.name: Row-picture lock-in
misconceptions.description: Sees a system only as intersecting lines, never as a combination of columns.

misconceptions.name: Always-unique belief
misconceptions.description: Assumes every system has exactly one solution.

misconceptions.name: Shape-equals-solvability
misconceptions.description: Concludes solvability from the count of equations and unknowns alone.

misconceptions.name: Illegal row operation
misconceptions.description: Multiplies a row by zero or adds a row to itself without scaling.

misconceptions.name: REF and RREF confusion
misconceptions.description: Stops at row echelon form or believes RREF is not unique.

misconceptions.name: Free-variable mishandling
misconceptions.description: Fails to identify free variables or parameterizes with the wrong variable.

misconceptions.name: Inconsistent-homogeneous error
misconceptions.description: Believes a homogeneous system can have no solution.

misconceptions.name: Count-based span
misconceptions.description: Believes span depends on the number of vectors rather than their independence.

misconceptions.name: Scalar-sign confusion
misconceptions.description: Mishandles the geometric effect of a negative scalar.

items.stem: Solve the system: 2x + y = 5 and x - y = 1. Give x and y.
Solve the seeded 3 by 3 integer system for x1, x2, x3.
Row reduce the matrix to reduced row echelon form.
Classify the solution set of: x + y = 2 and 2x + 2y = 4.
Write the general solution of: x1 + x2 = 2 and x3 = 5 in parametric form.
